task_completion.description: Document the six months of the billing period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet or document lists the months from January to June (or Jan-Jun).

task_completion.description: Document the electricity usage and cost for each month.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The correct usage (kWh) and cost ($) values are entered for each month (e.g., Jan: 850 kWh / $102.00, May: 1420 kWh / $170.40).

task_completion.description: Perform analysis to find the anomaly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are calculations visible, such as a column for 'Cost per kWh' showing $0.12, an 'Average' calculation showing ~928.33 kWh, or percentage deviations.
The month of May is explicitly identified as the anomaly (e.g., labeled with the word 'Anomaly', 'High', or a specific note).

The row, cells, or text corresponding to the May data are visually highlighted using formatting tools (e.g., a distinct background fill color, red text, or bolding) to stand out from the other months.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI application to document and highlight the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) or a graphical word processor being used, rather than just terminal output.

integrity.description: The highlighting was done using actual formatting tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The highlight is a true visual format (like cell background color or font color) applied via the application's UI, not just typing the word '*highlight*' next to the data.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the application to enter data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data appears in the application's grid or document body, indicating it was typed or pasted into the software rather than generated purely via a background script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct billing data is: January (850 kWh, $102.00), February (780 kWh, $93.60), March (820 kWh, $98.40), April (890 kWh, $106.80), May (1420 kWh, $170.40), June (810 kWh, $97.20). The cost per kWh is exactly $0.12 for all months. The average usage is 928.33 kWh. The anomaly is May, which has a usage of 1420 kWh (52.96% above average) and a cost of $170.40. The agent must document this data, analyze it, identify May as the anomaly, and visually highlight the May entry.
